name: Panda3D (3D Game Engine in Python)
pricing_notes: Open source (BSD-3-Clause); Carnegie Mellon / Disney Research

performance.notes: C++ rendering engine; 60fps for typical 3D scenes with OpenGL/DirectX

agent_readiness.notes: Full 3D game engine scriptable in Python. from direct.showbase.ShowBase import ShowBase. class App(ShowBase): def __init__(self): super().__init__(); self.scene = self.loader.loadModel('models/environment'); self.scene.reparentTo(self.render). app = App(); app.run(). NodePath: node = loader.loadModel('cube.egg'); node.setPos(0, 10, 0); node.setHpr(0, 45, 0); node.setScale(2). Textures: node.setTexture(tex). Physics: from panda3d.bullet import BulletWorld. Collision: fromNode.node().addSolid(CollisionBox). Tasks: taskMgr.add(update_task). Events: accept('arrow_left', move_left). Audio: self.audio = self.loader.loadSfx('sound.wav'). Particles. Shaders. Networking.

gotchas: EGG/BAM model formats non-standard — must convert from Blender/FBX
ShowBase.run() blocks — not compatible with async event loops
No type annotations — poor IDE support
Coordinate system uses Y-forward Z-up (right-hand) — different from many tools
best_when: When full OpenGL/DirectX 3D engine with Python control is needed
avoid_when: Production AAA games (use Unity or Unreal Engine)
summary: Panda3D is a full 3D game engine scriptable in Python. ShowBase: loadModel(), reparentTo(render). NodePath: setPos(), setHpr(), setScale(), setTexture(). Bullet physics integration. CollisionSystem. taskMgr for game loop. Events and input. Audio. Particles. Shaders. BSD-3 by Carnegie Mellon/Disney. Best for Python-scriptable 3D simulations and research. Non-standard EGG model format. ShowBase.run() blocks async. No type annotations.
